title: Consumer Stress Spike From Weekly Google Trends On Debt Consolidation Searches
thesis: A sudden rise in debt consolidation interest signals rising consumer financial stress, often preceding reduced discretionary spending. Consumer discretionary sector suffers when consumers increase debt management concerns.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'debt consolidation'
signal_logic: Enter short XLY if weekly search interest rises more than 15% compared to prior 4-week average
entry_exit: Entry: Enter short XLY if weekly search interest rises more than 15% compared to prior 4-week average Exit: Exit after 3 weeks or if interest falls below 10% above 4-week average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for 'debt consolidation' via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly Google Trends data often shows spikes in consumer stress terms several times a year.

schema.idea: Consumer stress spike from weekly Google Trends on 'debt consolidation' searches

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'debt consolidation'

schema.behavior: A sudden rise in debt consolidation interest signals rising consumer financial stress, often preceding reduced discretionary spending.

market_impact.rationale: Consumer discretionary sector suffers when consumers increase debt management concerns.

trade_logic.entry: Enter short XLY if weekly search interest rises more than 15% compared to prior 4-week average
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 3 weeks or if interest falls below 10% above 4-week average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly Google Trends data often shows spikes in consumer stress terms several times a year.

title: Macro Input Pressure Rapid Increase In Natural Gas Futures Volatility Signals Energy Sector Rotation
thesis: Spikes in natural gas volatility often precede energy sector price swings and rotation opportunities. Energy sector volatility can trigger price rallies as markets price supply shocks.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily price and volatility for natural gas futures
signal_logic: Enter long XLE if daily volatility rises more than 25% day-over-day and closes above 5-day volatility average
entry_exit: Entry: Enter long XLE if daily volatility rises more than 25% day-over-day and closes above 5-day volatility average Exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if volatility falls below 5-day average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily price and volatility for natural gas futures via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Energy volatility spikes multiple times per year driven by weather and geopolitical events.

schema.idea: Macro input pressure: rapid increase in natural gas futures volatility signals energy sector rotation

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily price and volatility for natural gas futures

schema.behavior: Spikes in natural gas volatility often precede energy sector price swings and rotation opportunities.

market_impact.rationale: Energy sector volatility can trigger price rallies as markets price supply shocks.

trade_logic.entry: Enter long XLE if daily volatility rises more than 25% day-over-day and closes above 5-day volatility average
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if volatility falls below 5-day average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Energy volatility spikes multiple times per year driven by weather and geopolitical events.

title: Freight Logistics Squeeze From Weekly Fred Producer Price Index For Transportation Rising Faster Than Industrial Ppi
thesis: Rising transport PPI relative to industrial PPI signals cost pressure on logistics that may squeeze manufacturing margins. Industrial sector profitability pressured by rising freight costs.
data_sources: FRED API: PPI Transportation and PPI Industrial
signal_logic: Enter short XLI if Transportation PPI increases more than 1% week-over-week and exceeds Industrial PPI by 0.5%
entry_exit: Entry: Enter short XLI if Transportation PPI increases more than 1% week-over-week and exceeds Industrial PPI by 0.5% Exit: Exit after 4 weeks or when spread narrows below 0.3%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED API: PPI Transportation and PPI Industrial via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly PPI data often shows transportation cost spikes aligned with supply chain disruptions.

schema.idea: Freight logistics squeeze from weekly FRED Producer Price Index for transportation rising faster than industrial PPI

data.source: FRED API: PPI Transportation and PPI Industrial

schema.behavior: Rising transport PPI relative to industrial PPI signals cost pressure on logistics that may squeeze manufacturing margins.

market_impact.rationale: Industrial sector profitability pressured by rising freight costs.

trade_logic.entry: Enter short XLI if Transportation PPI increases more than 1% week-over-week and exceeds Industrial PPI by 0.5%
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 4 weeks or when spread narrows below 0.3%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly PPI data often shows transportation cost spikes aligned with supply chain disruptions.

title: Political Insider Selling Spike In Financial Sector Etfs
thesis: Surge in insider selling in financial sector ETFs often precedes sector weakness due to anticipated regulatory or economic headwinds. Insider selling signals reduced confidence in financial sector outlook.
data_sources: Political insider filings aggregated weekly (public scrape)
signal_logic: Enter short XLF if insider selling volume exceeds average by 30% over past 2 weeks
entry_exit: Entry: Enter short XLF if insider selling volume exceeds average by 30% over past 2 weeks Exit: Exit after 5 weeks or if insider selling volume normalizes
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Political insider filings frequently spike with sector regulatory fears.

title: Labor Jobs Surge In Temporary Help Services Signals Upcoming Strength In Industrial Sector
thesis: Rising temporary help employment often leads industrial production before permanent hires catch up. Temporary jobs growth indicates rising industrial activity and capacity ramp-up.
data_sources: FRED API: Temporary Help Services Employment
signal_logic: Enter long XLI if temporary help employment grows >0.5% week-over-week for two consecutive weeks
entry_exit: Entry: Enter long XLI if temporary help employment grows >0.5% week-over-week for two consecutive weeks Exit: Exit after 6 weeks or if growth turns negative for a week
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED API: Temporary Help Services Employment via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Temporary help employment data releases weekly and often show meaningful growth spurts.
